_meta.method: Browser crawl via Claude in Chrome MCP
_meta.note: Pricing rows are raw text exactly as displayed on the page. Each row contains an area/variant label and one or more price values. Recommend parsing into structured fields (variant, mode, price, savings) at the seed-data stage.

20 cm2 - 5cm x 4cm $100 Approximate size of a matchbox

100 cm2 - 10cm x 10cm $170 Approximate size of a passport

tattoo-removal._note: UNIQUE pricing model: by tattoo size in cm². 9 size tiers. Each comes with a real-world comparison (coin, matchbox, A4 page). Schema must support free-form variant labels and helpful 'size_comparison' field.

schema_implications.1: pack_size varies per service: null (no packs), 3, 4, or 6. Must be per-service column, not a global constant.
schema_implications.2: Some services have NO pack pricing (fibroblast-plasma, tattoo-removal-with-saline-solution, tattoo-removal).
schema_implications.3: Pricing variants are free-form labels — must be TEXT, not an enum of areas. Examples: 'Saddle Bags', 'Pelvic floor', '4 cm2 - 2cm x 2cm', 'Vitamin A - Face only'.
schema_implications.4: Medi-Aesthetic Peels uses a `variant_group` concept (Vitamin A vs TCA 15% vs Tretinoin) — could be modeled as a nested grouping or just a prefix in the label.
schema_implications.5: 'From $X' pricing exists — need a `is_starting_price` boolean column or store price as text.
schema_implications.6: Recommended for: not present on every service (e.g. Radiofrequency has none scraped). Field must be nullable.
schema_implications.8: Some services note medical clearance is required (Fibroblast skin tags) — surface this as a `requires_medical_clearance` flag or as part of the variant note.
